source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults with acute or chronic rhinosinusitis; the official web-page target-age wording is internally inconsistent and is retained as an applicability limitation.
source.setting: Primary, urgent, outpatient, and ENT assessment and review of adult rhinosinusitis.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult acute-sinusitis symptom pattern, reassessment, chronic-rhinosinusitis objective confirmation, modifying factors, polyps, and clinician-reviewed allergy or immune testing. It does not diagnose bacterial infection, recommend antibiotics, or order imaging.

exact_location.heading: Key action statement 6 — treatment-failure or worsening reassessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports interval change, persistent or worsening symptom review, clinician reassessment, and documented follow-up without changing treatment.

items.final_wording: Supports interval change, persistent or worsening symptom review, clinician reassessment, and documented follow-up without changing treatment.

exact_location.heading: Key action statement 6 — treatment-failure or worsening reassessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports interval change, persistent or worsening symptom review, clinician reassessment, and documented follow-up without changing treatment.

items.rationale: The exact reassessment statement supports interval change, symptom impact, associated or negative features, concerns, and clinician reassessment without declaring treatment failure.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation

exact_location.heading: Key action statements 7A–10 — chronic distinction, objective confirmation, modifiers, testing, and polyps

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

items.rationale: The exact chronic-sinusitis section supports clinician-performed ENT assessment, reviewed imaging or specialist reports, modifying-factor context, and polyp documentation without interpreting CT or ordering endoscopy.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults with acute or chronic rhinosinusitis; the official web-page target-age wording is internally inconsistent and is retained as an applicability limitation.
source.setting: Primary, urgent, outpatient, and ENT assessment and review of adult rhinosinusitis.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult acute-sinusitis symptom pattern, reassessment, chronic-rhinosinusitis objective confirmation, modifying factors, polyps, and clinician-reviewed allergy or immune testing. It does not diagnose bacterial infection, recommend antibiotics, or order imaging.

exact_location.heading: Key action statement 6 — treatment-failure or worsening reassessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports interval change, persistent or worsening symptom review, clinician reassessment, and documented follow-up without changing treatment.

added_items.rationale: The exact reassessment statement supports interval change, symptom impact, associated or negative features, concerns, and clinician reassessment without declaring treatment failure.

added_items.final_wording: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults with acute or chronic rhinosinusitis; the official web-page target-age wording is internally inconsistent and is retained as an applicability limitation.
source.setting: Primary, urgent, outpatient, and ENT assessment and review of adult rhinosinusitis.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult acute-sinusitis symptom pattern, reassessment, chronic-rhinosinusitis objective confirmation, modifying factors, polyps, and clinician-reviewed allergy or immune testing. It does not diagnose bacterial infection, recommend antibiotics, or order imaging.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements 7A–10 — chronic distinction, objective confirmation, modifiers, testing, and polyps

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

added_items.rationale: The exact chronic-sinusitis section supports clinician-performed ENT assessment, reviewed imaging or specialist reports, modifying-factor context, and polyp documentation without interpreting CT or ordering endoscopy.

added_items.final_wording: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults with acute or chronic rhinosinusitis; the official web-page target-age wording is internally inconsistent and is retained as an applicability limitation.
source.setting: Primary, urgent, outpatient, and ENT assessment and review of adult rhinosinusitis.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult acute-sinusitis symptom pattern, reassessment, chronic-rhinosinusitis objective confirmation, modifying factors, polyps, and clinician-reviewed allergy or immune testing. It does not diagnose bacterial infection, recommend antibiotics, or order imaging.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements 1A–1B — acute rhinosinusitis distinction and clinical diagnosis context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.

added_items.rationale: The exact symptom-pattern section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions only when documented; it does not label bacterial disease or choose an antibiotic.
